title: SpaceX's listing stirs up social media hype, ticker bets
author: ET
short_desc: Traders are betting thousands of ​dollars on the company's ticker and speculating over its entry into the most elite club of US companies, giving the world's most valuable startup a level of social media buzz that only a few companies enjoy, especially when they are yet to file ‌their IPO paperwork.
long_desc: From rocket launches drawing millions of YouTube views to social media frenzy over âits potential listing, SpaceX's debut is shaping âup to be a landmark moment for Wall Street.Traders are betting thousands of âdollars on the company's ticker and speculating over its entry into the most elite club of US companies, giving the world's most valuable startup a level of social media buzz that only a few companies enjoy, especially when they are yet to file âtheir IPO paperwork. On â Polymarket, users â were betting on topics including the company's targeted valuation, the exchange it will list on and the ticker its shares would trade under. âThe combined trading volume of such bets exceeded more than $15.2 million, as of Friday.Odds on the prediction markets platform put âa 25% chance on SpaceX choosing the letter "X" as its ticker, a sharp drop from 60% a month ago. The single-letter ticker is up for grabs after U.S. Steel, which reportedly held it for over a century, delisted âfrom the New York Stock Exchange after being bought by Japan's â Nippon Steel last âyear.Musk's social media platform is also called X after a rebrand from Twitter in â2023.Tuttle Capital Management âCEO Matthew Tuttle said a better alternative would be "SPCX" - also the ticker of an â exchange traded fund his company manages.Tuttle has indicated openness to selling the âSPCX symbol to SpaceX. "I've not heard from Elon, but my phone line âis still open and I'm holding out hope…

title: In wake of US social media verdicts, look at what limits other countries have imposed for kids
author: ET
short_desc: On Wednesday, a Los Angeles jury found both Meta and YouTube liable for harms to children using their services. A day earlier in New Mexico, a jury determined that Meta knowingly harmed children's mental health and concealed what it knew about child sexual exploitation on its platforms.
long_desc: Dual jury verdicts this week have validated longstanding concerns about the dangers of social media for young people. But the US lacks federal regulation that meaningfully addresses these harms.On Wednesday, a Los Angeles jury found both Meta and YouTube liable for harms to children using their services. A day earlier in New Mexico, a jury determined that Meta knowingly harmed children's mental health and concealed what it knew about child sexual exploitation on its platforms.Although parents and children's advocates cheered the decisions, they argue that without federal regulation to rein in the companies, platforms like Instagram, YouTube and TikTok are unlikely to implement any meaningful change. Many are pinning their hopes on the Kids Online Safety Act, a bill aimed at protecting kids from the harms of social media, gaming sites and other online platforms. It won Senate approval in 2024 but has lingered since.Other countries, meanwhile, have implemented - or are planning - a bevy of restrictions on children's online activities, ranging from social media bans to requiring younger teens to link their accounts to a parent's. Here's a look at how countries outside the United States are regulating kids and technology. Australia bans kids under 16 from social media  In 2024, Australia became the first country to kick kids under 16 off social media. The law makes platforms - including TikTok, Facebook, Snapchat, Reddit, X and Instagram - liable for fines of up to 50 million…

title: Austria says will ban social media for children under 14
author: ET
short_desc: Austrian news agency APA said on Friday that Austria is going to ban social media for children up to the age of 14 following lengthy negotiations within the ruling coalition.
long_desc: Austria is going to ban social media for children up to the age of 14 following lengthy negotiations within the ruling coalition, the Austrian news agency APA said Friday. The details of this ban will be fleshed out at a press conference later Friday, the government said in a statement.

The bridge loan âis unsecured, the company said.

title: WhatsApp update: AI will now write your messages, touch up photos within chat
author: ET
short_desc: According to the company, even for those using the Writing Help option, the chats will remain private. The update will let users manage storage, move chats between phones and use two WhatsApp accounts on one phone.
long_desc: WhatsApp is updating its platform with a bunch of new tools and features, including artificial intelligence (AI)-powered suggestions that make replying quicker and easier. Hereâs what users can expect.AI willâ¦finish your sentencesA notable change comes to WhatsAppâs âWriting Helpâ feature, first launched last August. It already lets users rephrase, proofread, or tweak the tone of messages.Now, AI will suggest complete replies based on your conversation, helping you get your message âjust right,â according to its blog. Meta is hoping people use this in-app tool rather than external apps like ChatGPT.The company claims that the chats will remain private even if people use Writing Help.Touch up photos in chatUsers can now make photo edits directly in WhatsApp using Meta AI. author: ET
short_desc: The state IT/BT minister’s X post comes ahead of the release of a joint report by Nasscom and Zinnov, highlighting strong momentum in India’s startup ecosystem despite a more selective funding environment. Per the report, Bengaluru accounted for 28% of India’s deeptech startups as of 2025, followed
long_desc: Karnataka continues to be the countryâs most preferred destination for technology startups to scale, the stateâs IT/BT minister Priyank Kharge said on Thursday.âKarnataka has always stayed ahead of the curve in technology. Our policy clarity and targeted fund backing continue to make it the best destination in the country for tech startups to scale,â Kharge said in a post on X.The statement comes ahead of the release of a joint report by Nasscom and Zinnov, which highlights strong momentum in Indiaâs startup ecosystem despite a more selective funding environment.According to the report, Indian tech startups raised $9.1 billion in 2025, marking a 23% year-on-year (YoY) increase.The report further said that Bengaluru accounted for 28% of Indiaâs deeptech startups as of 2025,…
